examples.question: What is the total number of patients screened for blood pressure?
examples.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT patient_id) AS total_screened FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE avg_systolic IS NOT NULL AND avg_diastolic IS NOT NULL
examples.description: Count distinct patients with BP readings

examples.question: Show the total population screened for BP
examples.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_screened FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE avg_systolic IS NOT NULL OR avg_diastolic IS NOT NULL
examples.description: Total records with any BP measurement

examples.question: How many patients have elevated blood pressure?
examples.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT patient_id) AS elevated_bp_count FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE avg_systolic >= 140 OR avg_diastolic >= 90
examples.description: Count patients with high BP (systolic >= 140 or diastolic >= 90)

examples.question: What is the average systolic blood pressure by gender?
examples.sql: SELECT gender, AVG(avg_systolic) AS avg_systolic, COUNT(*) AS patient_count FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE avg_systolic IS NOT NULL GROUP BY gender
examples.description: Average systolic BP grouped by gender

examples.question: Show blood pressure distribution by age group
examples.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN age < 30 THEN 'Under 30' WHEN age BETWEEN 30 AND 50 THEN '30-50' WHEN age BETWEEN 51 AND 70 THEN '51-70' ELSE 'Over 70' END AS age_group, AVG(avg_systolic) AS avg_systolic, AVG(avg_diastolic) AS avg_diastolic, COUNT(*) AS patient_count FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE age IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
examples.description: BP stats by age brackets using CASE expression

examples.question: Compare initial and latest blood pressure readings for patients
examples.sql: WITH ranked_readings AS (SELECT patient_id, avg_systolic, avg_diastolic, created_at, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY patient_id ORDER BY created_at ASC) AS first_rank, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY patient_id ORDER BY created_at DESC) AS last_rank FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE avg_systolic IS NOT NULL) SELECT f.patient_id, f.avg_systolic AS initial_systolic, l.avg_systolic AS latest_systolic, l.avg_systolic - f.avg_systolic AS systolic_change FROM ranked_readings f JOIN ranked_readings l ON f.patient_id = l.patient_id WHERE f.first_rank = 1 AND l.last_rank = 1 LIMIT 100
examples.description: Compare first and last BP readings using ROW_NUMBER in CTE

examples.question: Find patients with consecutive high blood pressure readings
examples.sql: WITH numbered AS (SELECT patient_id, avg_systolic, created_at, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY patient_id ORDER BY created_at) AS rn FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE avg_systolic >= 140), grouped AS (SELECT patient_id, avg_systolic, created_at, rn, rn - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY patient_id ORDER BY created_at) AS grp FROM numbered) SELECT patient_id, COUNT(*) AS consecutive_high_readings, MIN(created_at) AS streak_start, MAX(created_at) AS streak_end FROM grouped GROUP BY patient_id, grp HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 ORDER BY consecutive_high_readings DESC
examples.description: Detect consecutive high BP readings using ROW_NUMBER difference technique

examples.question: What is the monthly trend of patient screenings?
examples.sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month, COUNT(DISTINCT patient_id) AS patients_screened, AVG(avg_systolic) AS avg_systolic FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

examples.question: Show the latest reading for each patient
examples.sql: WITH latest AS (SELECT patient_id, avg_systolic, avg_diastolic, created_at, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY patient_id ORDER BY created_at DESC) AS rn FROM clinical_data_latest) SELECT patient_id, avg_systolic, avg_diastolic, created_at FROM latest WHERE rn = 1 LIMIT 100
examples.description: Get most recent reading per patient using ROW_NUMBER in CTE

examples.question: Count patients by risk level
examples.sql: SELECT risk_level, COUNT(DISTINCT patient_id) AS patient_count FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE risk_level IS NOT NULL GROUP BY risk_level ORDER BY patient_count DESC

examples.question: Find patients screened in the last 90 days
examples.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT patient_id) AS recent_patients FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'
examples.description: Count patients screened in last 90 days using INTERVAL syntax

examples.question: What percentage of patients have hypertension?
examples.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN avg_systolic >= 140 OR avg_diastolic >= 90 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS hypertension_percentage FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE avg_systolic IS NOT NULL
examples.description: Calculate percentage with hypertension using CASE WHEN

examples.question: Show top 10 sites by patient count
examples.sql: SELECT site_id, COUNT(DISTINCT patient_id) AS patient_count FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE site_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY site_id ORDER BY patient_count DESC LIMIT 10
examples.description: Top sites ranked by patient volume

examples.question: Calculate BMI statistics by gender
examples.sql: SELECT gender, AVG(bmi) AS avg_bmi, MIN(bmi) AS min_bmi, MAX(bmi) AS max_bmi, COUNT(*) AS count FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE bmi IS NOT NULL AND gender IS NOT NULL GROUP BY gender

examples.question: Find patients with glucose readings above normal
examples.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT patient_id) AS high_glucose_patients FROM clinical_data_latest WHERE glucose_value > 100
